content.full_text:
July 15, 1988


William E. McBee
Cupertino Sanitary District
Santa Clara County
20065 Stevens Creek Blvd., Bldg. C
Cupertino, CA 95014

                                          Re:  Your Request for Advice
                                               Our File No. A-88-236

Dear Mr. McBee:

     This is in response to your letter requesting advice
regarding application of the new restrictions on
publicly-funded mass mailings under Proposition 73.

     Enclosed are copies of our first advice letters
interpreting the mass mailing restricts.  After examining the
new statute and the analysis by the Legislative Analyst, we
have concluded that the voters intended to restrict mailings by
state and local elected officials, but did not intend to ban
publicly funded mailings entirely.

     Upon review of the two documents you have submitted to us,
the letter to the property owners, signed by the District
Manager-Engineer, appears to be in compliance with Proposition
73.  However, concerning the second document, I would like to
direct your attention to the restrictions imposed on
periodicals and annual reports (Item g, page 3 of June 16th
letter).  You will note that annual reports which include an
elected officer's name, signature or photograph are prohibited.

     In the future, we expect to address additional questions
concerning the mass mailing restrictions in Proposition 73, and
we have included your name on our mailing list for information
on the subject.

                                          Sincerely,

                                          Diana M. Griffiths
                                          General Counsel

                                          [signed]
                                          By: Lilly Spitz
                                              Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:LS:plh
Enclosures

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

CUPERTINO SANITARY DISTRICT
SANTA CLARA COUNTY

DISTRICT MANAGER-ENGINEER
MARK THOMAS & CO INC.

20065 STEVENS CREEK BOULEVARD, BLDG C
CUPERTINO, CA 95014
(408) 253-7071

BOARD OF DIRECTORS
ROY M RUSHTON Ph.D.
WENDELL M KERN JR. M.D.
CURTIS B HARRISON
DR. DONALD R. FREYTAG
RICHARD A FELLOWS

DISTRICT COUNSEL
PHILIP D. ASSAF OF
STURGIS, NESS, BRUNSWELL &
SPERRY

June 21, 1988
File: CuSD SP-159
SP-121

California Fair Political
Practices Commission
428 J. Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Gentlemen:

The Board of Directors of the Cupertino Sanitary District have
requested that we submit to your office for review, the two (2)
enclosed documents, to determine if either appears to be in
violation of Proposition 73.

1. Draft copy of a letter, on District letterhead,
   proposed to be mailed to a selected group of
   property owners in the District, outlining
   District standards and policies for maintenance
   of sanitary sewers.

2. A copy of the District's annual report for
   fiscal year 1987-88, mailed to all citizens
   of the District. The District has mailed a
   similar type report each year since 1962.

Your opinion as to whether either of the above could be in
conflict with the provisions of Proposition 73 would be
appreciated.

Yours very truly,

MARK THOMAS & CO. INC.
District Manager-Engineer

[signature]

by William E. McBee

WEM:lw
Enclosures


SUPPLYING SANITARY SEWERAGE SERVICES FOR:
CITY OF CUPERTINO, PORTIONS OF THE CITIES OF SARATOGA, SUNNYVALE AND LOS ALTOS
AND SURROUNDING UNINCORPORATED AREAS

DRAFT

CUPERTINO SANITARY DISTRICT
SANTA CLARA COUNTY

DISTRICT MANAGER-ENGINEER
MARK THOMAS & CO INC.

DISTRICT COUNSEL
PHILIP D. ASSAF OF
STURGIS, NESS, BRUNSWELL &
SPERRY

20065 STEVENS CREEK BOULEVARD BLDG C
CUPERTINO, CA 95014
(408) 253-7071

BOARD OF DIRECTORS
ROY M RUSHTON JR (PRES)
WENDELL W KERR JR (SECY)
CURTIS B HARRISON
DR JOSEPH F BROWN
RICHARD A FELLOWS

June , 1988
File: CuSD SP-159 (79074)
CuSD SP-121 (73122)

TO PROPERTY OWNERS:

The Cupertino Sanitary District acquired the operation
and maintenance responsibilities for the sanitary sewer
collection system in your area in 1979.

It is the policy of the District to maintain all sanitary
sewers within the public streets and easements. This
includes the street portion of the individual sanitary
sewer house lateral, except when there is no property line
cleanout which is accessible or serviceable.

However, the installation of a property line cleanout
may not have been a requirement when your sewer line was
originally installed. The District recognizes this fact
and would like to take this opportunity to provide you
with information necessary to install a property line
cleanout at this time.

Additionally, if the elevation of your floor is below
the adjacent street, it may be necessary to install a
standard backflow protective device.

Enclosed is a sketch showing a typical house lateral
connection to a cleanout, and a street lateral from the
cleanout to the main street sewer. Also enclosed is a
sketch showing a standard backflow protective device
installation.

As the District will only maintain those laterals
within the public street or easement, it is important
that each property owner check to see if there is an
existing cleanout at or near their property line. The
installation of this cleanout and standard backflow
protective device, if required, is the responsibility
of the property owner.

SUPPLYING SANITARY SEWERAGE SERVICES FOR:
CITY OF CUPERTINO, PORTIONS OF THE CITIES OF SARATOGA, SUNNYVALE AND LOS ALTOS
AND SURROUNDING UNINCORPORATED AREAS

PROPERTY OWNERS                2                June  , 1988

If you should have any questions, need information
regarding the location of the sanitary sewer lateral,
or are uncertain about your particular requirements,
please call the District Office at 253-7071 or stop in
at 20065 Stevens Creek Boulevard, Building C, Cupertino,
California.

                                        Yours very truly,

                                        MARK THOMAS & CO. INC.
                                        District Manager-Engineer


                                        Phillip R. Savio

PRS:lw
Enc. 2

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 22, 1988


William E. McBee
Cupertino Sanitary District
20065 Stevens Creek Blvd., Bldg. C
Cupertino, CA 95014

Re: 88-236

Dear Mr. McBee:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on June 22, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact Lilly Spitz, an attorney in the
Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to information needed. If your request is for
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can.
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec.
18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

sections.conclusion: Upon review of the two documents you have submitted to us, the letter to the property owners, signed by the District Manager-Engineer, appears to be in compliance with proposition 73. sections.question_synthetic: Whether two documents submitted by Cupertino Sanitary District—a letter to property owners regarding sewer maintenance policies and an annual report—comply with Proposition 73's restrictions on publicly-funded mass mailings.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the letter to property owners complies with Proposition 73, but the annual report violates the restrictions if it includes an elected officer's name, signature, or photograph. The FPPC clarified that Proposition 73 restricts but does not entirely ban publicly-funded mailings by elected officials.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request letter (June 21, 1988) and FPPC response letters (June 22 and July 15, 1988). The July 15 letter serves as the main advice letter with conclusion section. Question inferred from incoming request as formal question section not explicitly labeled. High confidence based on clear structure despite informal formatting noted in parsing instructions.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether two documents submitted by Cupertino Sanitary District—a letter to property owners regarding sewer maintenance policies and an annual report—comply with Proposition 73's restrictions on publicly-funded mass mailings.

CONCLUSION: Upon review of the two documents you have submitted to us, the letter to the property owners, signed by the District Manager-Engineer, appears to be in compliance with proposition 73. However, concerning the second document, I would like to direct your attention to the restrictions imposed on periodicals and annual reports (Item g, page 3 of June 16th letter). You will note that annual reports which include an elected officer's name, signature or photograph are prohibited.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responded to Cupertino Sanitary District's request for advice on Proposition 73 compliance for two documents: a letter regarding sewer maintenance policies and an annual report. The FPPC approved the letter but flagged the annual report as potentially non-compliant if it included elected officers' names, signatures, or photographs.
